name: Taurine
mechanism: Conjugates bile acids, supports bile flow, GABAergic calming, membrane stabilization
notes: Split AM/PM for tolerability

name: MSM
mechanism: Sulfur donor, anti-inflammatory, connective tissue support
notes: Requires molybdenum

name: Molybdenum
mechanism: Cofactor for sulfite oxidase, supports sulfur metabolism
notes: Avoid very high doses

name: Psyllium Husk
mechanism: Soluble fiber, binds bile-bound toxins and endotoxin, improves stool form
notes: Separate from meds/supps by 1–2 h

name: Calcium D-Glucarate
mechanism: Supports glucuronidation and reduces enterohepatic estrogen recirculation
notes: Useful for high estrogen or cholestasis tendency

name: PHGG
mechanism: Partially hydrolyzed guar gum; feeds beneficial bacteria, improves motility and reduces histamine
notes: Titrate slowly to avoid gas

name: MegaSporeBiotic
mechanism: Spore-based probiotic to reseed microbiome and improve gut barrier
notes: Start with ½ cap to avoid herx

name: Carrot Salad
mechanism: Raw carrot fiber plus oil and acid; binds endotoxin and bile, antifungal and anti-estrogenic
notes: Finely shredded with oil + vinegar/lemon

name: NAC
mechanism: Glutathione precursor, mucolytic, disrupts biofilms
notes: Take away from metals

name: Creatine Monohydrate
mechanism: Increases phosphocreatine stores, improves ATP recycling, supports dopamine and strength
notes: With food or anytime

name: L-Carnitine L-Tartrate
mechanism: Transports fatty acids into mitochondria, increases androgen receptor density in muscle
notes: Best with meals

name: HMB
mechanism: Metabolite of leucine supporting muscle protein synthesis and reducing breakdown
notes: Useful during high training

name: Benfotiamine
mechanism: Supports PDH activity and carbohydrate metabolism; improves endothelial function
notes: Baseline metabolic ignition

name: ALCAR
mechanism: Acetyl-L-carnitine; supports mitochondrial function and nerve conduction
notes: May be mildly stimulating

name: Alpha-Lipoic Acid
mechanism: Lipoic acid; regenerates other antioxidants and improves insulin sensitivity
notes: Take with food if sensitive

name: Vitamin C
mechanism: Ascorbate; antioxidant, supports collagen and endothelial health, NO recycling
notes: Divide doses if needed

name: Zinc
mechanism: Cofactor for many enzymes, supports testosterone, dopamine and immune function
notes: Avoid chronic very high doses

name: Selenium
mechanism: Essential for thyroid deiodinases and glutathione peroxidase
notes: Watch upper limit

name: Magnesium Glycinate
mechanism: Magnesium chelate; supports ATP, relaxation, sleep and insulin sensitivity
notes: PM preferred

name: Vitamin K2
mechanism: Directs calcium to bone, protects vasculature; synergistic with vitamin D and aspirin
notes: Use especially with aspirin

name: Omega-3 (EPA/DHA)
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory, resolves neuroinflammation, supports endothelial function
notes: Best with food and vitamin E

name: Aspirin (low-dose)
mechanism: Irreversible COX inhibition; lowers inflammation, improves endothelial function, sensitizes thyroid
notes: Monitor GI tolerance and bleeding risk

name: Nattokinase
mechanism: Fibrinolytic enzyme from natto; improves blood flow and reduces clot burden
notes: Avoid with anticoagulants

name: L-Citrulline
mechanism: Precursor to arginine and NO; improves endothelial function and erection quality
notes: Pre-workout or pre-sex

name: Icariin
mechanism: Mild PDE5 inhibitor and NO booster from Epimedium; supports erections and vascular health
notes: Stack with citrulline/pycnogenol

name: Histidine
mechanism: Precursor for histamine; modulates VEGF and angiogenesis, supports endothelial adaptation
notes: Pre-vascular training

name: Hesperidin
mechanism: Improves capillary tone and venous return, synergistic with citrulline
notes: Pre Angion/sexual activity

name: Pycnogenol
mechanism: Maritime pine bark; improves NO signaling, reduces oxidative stress in endothelium
notes: Strong synergy with citrulline

name: Ecklonia cava
mechanism: Long half-life polyphenols; supports NO and microcirculation, mild PDE5 effects
notes: Pre-performance

name: Agmatine sulfate
mechanism: Decarboxylated arginine; neuromodulator (imidazoline receptors, NMDA/polyamine pathways) with variable NO/arginase signaling effects; may reduce sympathetic tone and support vascular responsiveness.
notes: Use PRN (pre-sex/pre-training). Watch for GI upset, headache, lightheadedness/low BP—especially if stacking with citrulline/tadalafil. Trial solo first.
role: PRN EQ/pump + pelvic relaxation; neuromodulatory support.

name: Beet Extract / Nitrate
mechanism: Nitrate source; increases NO and lowers blood pressure, improves endurance
notes: Pre-exertion

name: L-Arginine
mechanism: Direct substrate for NO synthase; may support erections in some
notes: Pre-workout/sex, less efficient than citrulline

name: Tongkat Ali
mechanism: Eurycoma longifolia; increases free testosterone, lowers SHBG, may improve libido and mood
notes: Cycle if needed

name: Cistanche
mechanism: Tonic herb that supports androgens, NO and mitochondrial output
notes: Titrate based on response

name: Proviron (Mesterolone)
mechanism: DHT-like androgen that does not convert to estrogen; increases libido, mood, hardness
notes: Off-label; monitor lipids and prostate

name: Pregnenolone
mechanism: Mother steroid; supports neurosteroidogenesis, mood, cognition and libido
notes: Morning preferred

name: DHEA
mechanism: Adrenal steroid; can convert to androgens and estrogens, supports neurosteroid tone
notes: Monitor for estrogenic sides

name: SAM-e
mechanism: Supports methylation, dopamine synthesis and liver function
notes: Take on empty stomach in AM

name: DHM (Dihydromyricetin)
mechanism: Flavonoid that modulates GABA-A and reduces alcohol toxicity; may support sleep and liver
notes: Evening use typical

name: Vitamin A (Retinol)
mechanism: Supports steroidogenesis, immune function and mucosal health
notes: Avoid early if PAS/PSSD-prone

name: Niacin (flush)
mechanism: Strong vasodilator, increases prostaglandins and may acutely alter dopamine/serotonin tone
notes: Monitor flush and liver at higher doses

name: BPC-157
mechanism: Pentadecapeptide with tissue-repair, angiogenic and anti-inflammatory properties
notes: Oral or subQ; cycle 2–4 weeks

name: Calcium D-Glucarate (CDG)
mechanism: Supports phase II glucuronidation and reduces enterohepatic recirculation of estrogens and toxins
notes: Often grouped with CAS-GUT-02; here logged explicitly

name: TUDCA
mechanism: Hydrophilic bile acid that protects hepatocytes and cholangiocytes, supports bile flow and reduces ER stress
notes: Use cautiously in suspected obstructive cholestasis

name: Betaine HCl
mechanism: Increases gastric acidity to improve protein digestion and reduce upper GI dysbiosis
notes: Titrate based on warmth/comfort; avoid with ulcers

name: Collagen / Gelatin
mechanism: Provides glycine and proline for connective tissue, gut lining and liver detox pathways
notes: Often taken in evening for sleep and tissue repair

name: NMN
mechanism: Nicotinamide mononucleotide; raises NAD+ to support mitochondrial function and sirtuin activity
notes: Take earlier in day

name: NR
mechanism: Nicotinamide riboside; alternative NAD+ precursor for mitochondrial support
notes: Not combined with NMN typically; choose one

name: AKG
mechanism: Alpha-ketoglutarate; supports Krebs cycle flux, ammonia handling and collagen turnover
notes: Often taken with vitamin C or protein

name: D-Ribose
mechanism: Pentose sugar used for ATP synthesis and cardiac/mitochondrial recovery
notes: Can lower blood sugar in some users

name: CoQ10
mechanism: Ubiquinone; part of electron transport chain, supports cardiac and endothelial function
notes: Take with fat-containing meal

name: PQQ
mechanism: Pyrroloquinoline quinone; promotes formation of new mitochondria and supports nerve function
notes: May be stimulating in some individuals

name: Shilajit
mechanism: Fulvic mineral complex; improves mitochondrial efficiency, testosterone utilization and nutrient transport
notes: Ensure purified, heavy metals screened

name: Apigenin
mechanism: Plant flavone that may reduce aromatase, support GABA-A and modulate SHBG
notes: High doses may be sedating

name: Bromantane
mechanism: Increases expression of tyrosine hydroxylase and dopamine; mild anxiolytic
notes: Cycle: e.g. 2 weeks on, 1 week off

name: 9-me-BC
mechanism: Beta-carboline derivative that may increase dopaminergic markers and motivation
notes: Experimental; monitor mood/anxiety

name: L-Tyrosine
mechanism: Precursor for dopamine, norepinephrine and thyroid hormone
notes: Best taken away from protein-heavy meals

name: Semax
mechanism: Nasal heptapeptide increasing BDNF and neuroprotection; may support olfaction
notes: Cycle use, avoid nightly chronic use

name: Selank
mechanism: Nasal peptide modulating GABA/glutamate, reducing anxiety and supporting cognition
notes: Synergistic with Semax

name: Dihexa
mechanism: Small molecule that may strongly potentiate HGF/c-Met and synaptogenesis
notes: Use with extreme caution; long half-life

name: NSI-189
mechanism: Experimental antidepressant increasing hippocampal volume and BDNF
notes: Cycle 4–12 weeks

name: ACD-856
mechanism: Enhances NGF/BDNF signaling; under investigation for cognitive disorders
notes: Highly experimental

name: Cerebrolysin
mechanism: Porcine brain-derived neuropeptide mixture supporting neuroregeneration and cognition
notes: Medical supervision recommended

name: Cortexin
mechanism: Brain-region-specific polypeptide complex used in some countries for cognition and neurorepair
notes: Region-specific protocols vary

name: Piracetam
mechanism: Enhances membrane fluidity and possibly neuroplasticity and microcirculation
notes: Often split doses

name: Phenylpiracetam
mechanism: Modified piracetam with psychostimulant and pro-motivation effects
notes: Cycle to avoid tolerance

name: Hydrogen Water Tablets
mechanism: Molecular hydrogen delivery for selective antioxidant and signaling effects
notes: Use on empty stomach for systemic effect

name: DIM
mechanism: Modulates estrogen metabolism and receptor balance
notes: May be too strong in low-estrogen states

name: I3C
mechanism: Cruciferous-derived compound altering estrogen metabolite ratios
notes: Can be rough on stomach in some

name: Vitamin D3
mechanism: Regulates calcium metabolism, steroidogenesis and immune function
notes: Monitor 25(OH)D levels

name: Vitamin E (mixed tocopherols)
mechanism: Protects PUFA in membranes, supports endocrine and cardiovascular health
notes: Use mixed tocopherols/tocotrienols

name: GHK-Cu
mechanism: Tripeptide with copper that promotes wound healing, collagen synthesis and tissue remodeling
notes: Monitor copper status if chronic

name: TB-500 (Thymosin Beta-4 fragment)
mechanism: Peptide fragment that may enhance tissue repair, angiogenesis and inflammation resolution
notes: Research compound; monitor for side effects

name: Kisspeptin-10
mechanism: Stimulates GnRH → LH/FSH → sex steroids; may reset hypothalamic axis
notes: Narrow therapeutic window

name: Oxytocin (intranasal)
mechanism: Modulates bonding, trust, tactile pleasure and sexual-affective circuits
notes: Chronic use may blunt natural dynamics

name: LSD
mechanism: 5-HT2A agonist producing large-scale neuroplasticity and potential 'reset' of limbic circuits
notes: Tool for IRM/Layer-0 work, not routine stack

name: Psilocybin
mechanism: Serotonergic psychedelic that can increase BDNF and alter emotional/sensory processing
notes: IRM/Consciousness tool

name: Raw Garlic / Allicin
mechanism: Broad-spectrum antimicrobial against bacteria and fungi; modulates biofilms
notes: Use short-term if tolerated

name: Oil of Oregano
mechanism: Potent antimicrobial and antifungal; disrupts biofilms
notes: Cycle and monitor gut tolerance

name: Neem
mechanism: Broad-spectrum anti-bacterial and anti-fungal herb
notes: Short cycles preferred

name: Caprylic Acid
mechanism: Medium-chain fatty acid targeting fungal membranes (e.g., Candida)
notes: Take with meals

name: Peppermint Oil (enteric)
mechanism: Enteric-coated oil with local antimicrobial and antispasmodic action
notes: Avoid in GERD if not enteric-coated

name: Rifaximin
mechanism: Non-systemic antibiotic for SIBO and IBS-related dysbiosis
notes: Medical supervision only

name: Neomycin
mechanism: Poorly absorbed aminoglycoside often combined with rifaximin for methane SIBO
notes: Supervised use only

name: Fluconazole
mechanism: Systemic triazole antifungal targeting Candida and other fungi
notes: Monitor liver enzymes

name: Nystatin
mechanism: Non-absorbed antifungal for GI Candida overgrowth
notes: Local GI effect; minimal absorption

name: Metronidazole
mechanism: Targets anaerobic bacteria and protozoa; used in some SIBO or parasitic protocols
notes: Supervised due to side effect profile

name: Tinidazole
mechanism: Longer half-life nitroimidazole for protozoal infections
notes: Supervised

name: Serrapeptase
mechanism: Systemic enzyme breaking down proteinaceous biofilms and inflammatory debris
notes: Monitor for bleeding if on anticoagulants

name: EDTA
mechanism: Binds divalent cations and disrupts some biofilms; also chelates metals
notes: Supervised; not routine

name: Bismuth Subsalicylate
mechanism: Coats GI mucosa, has mild antimicrobial and biofilm-disrupting properties
notes: Contains salicylate

name: SBO Probiotics (general)
mechanism: Spore-based organisms supporting robust microbiome and resilience
notes: Includes MegaSpore as a specific brand

name: Lactobacillus reuteri
mechanism: Strains such as DSM 17938 or ATCC 6475 may elevate oxytocin and affect social/sexual behavior
notes: Often used as yogurt or supplement

name: Inulin / Acacia Fiber
mechanism: Soluble fibers feeding Bifidobacteria and other beneficial species
notes: Titrate slowly

name: Vivomixx / VSL#3
mechanism: High-potency multi-strain probiotic for intensive microbiome support
notes: Best refrigerated

name: FMT (Fecal Microbiota Transplant)
mechanism: Transfer of donor microbiome to replace or augment host microbiota
notes: Highly experimental outside standard indications

name: Yohimbine
mechanism: Blocks presynaptic alpha-2 receptors, increasing sympathetic tone and penile blood flow
notes: Can cause anxiety, tachycardia; use with caution

name: Butea superba
mechanism: Thai herb with androgenic and PDE5-modulating properties
notes: Monitor for BP/headache

name: Tian Li
mechanism: Traditional formula marketed for libido and erectile function; multi-herb
notes: Quality varies; treat as experimental

name: Tadalafil
mechanism: Inhibits PDE5, raising cGMP and enhancing erectile response and endothelial function
notes: Watch BP, interactions with nitrates

name: Glycine
mechanism: Simple amino acid; supports collagen synthesis, liver detox and parasympathetic tone
notes: Often used at night for sleep

name: Berberine
mechanism: Isoquinoline alkaloid; AMPK activation, improved insulin sensitivity and endothelial signaling; antimicrobial against dysbiosis/SIBO patterns; can reduce endotoxin load indirectly supporting NO/vascular function.
notes: Use in cycles (e.g., 2–4 wks). Can cause cramps/constipation or die-off effects; interacts with some meds via CYP/P-gp; monitor BP if combined with vasodilators.
role: Metabolic + gut/kill-phase lever; indirect EQ support.
phase_dosage: P1.5: 300 mg with dinner; P2: 300–500 mg with meals 1–2×/day (cycle)

name: Mitolipin
mechanism: Cardiolipin-targeted phospholipid blend intended to restore mitochondrial inner membrane integrity and respiratory chain efficiency.
notes: Turnbuckle-associated Longecity compound; long-term mitochondrial repair strategy.

name: Succinate
mechanism: Provides substrate at complex II of the electron transport chain; supports ATP generation and can shift redox balance.
notes: Used in some mitochondrial stacks and Lostfalco-style protocols.

name: Oxaloacetate (OAA)
mechanism: Krebs cycle anaplerotic substrate; may modulate NAD+/NADH ratio and neuroprotection.
notes: Frequently paired with succinate/mitochondrial support cycles.

name: LL-37
mechanism: Endogenous cathelicidin-derived peptide with strong antimicrobial and immunomodulatory effects; used experimentally for microbiome and infection modulation.
dose_range: Dosing varies widely in research (e.g., 50–200 mcg/day) – experimental only
notes: Considered in microbiome reset strategies; high experimental risk.

name: Actovegin
mechanism: Deproteinized calf blood extract claimed to enhance oxygen utilization and glucose uptake in tissues.
dose_range: Per protocol (e.g., 2–5 mL IM a few times/week)
notes: Used in some Eastern European protocols for neuro and tissue repair.

name: Boron
mechanism: Influences steroid hormone metabolism, vitamin D status and inflammatory signaling.
notes: Discussion of borax/boron and SHBG modulation; monitor for toxicity at high doses.

name: Pancreatic Digestive Enzymes
mechanism: Lipase, protease and amylase mixture to support digestion, reduce undigested food burden and secondary dysbiosis.
notes: Used when intrinsic pancreatic enzyme output is suspected to be low.

name: BHB (Beta-Hydroxybutyrate)
mechanism: Alternative fuel for brain and muscle; modulates NAD+/NADH, lowers neuroinflammation and supports mitochondrial efficiency.
notes: Can lower appetite and alter electrolytes; monitor GI tolerance.

name: Fisetin
mechanism: Plant flavonoid with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and senolytic properties; may support cognition and vascular health.
dose_range: 100–500 mg/day or intermittent high-dose senolytic pulses
notes: Synergistic with quercetin-style stacks; experimental at high doses.

name: Jiaogulan (Gynostemma)
mechanism: AMPK-modulating and NO-supporting adaptogen; used for cardiovascular and metabolic resilience.
notes: May lower blood pressure and blood sugar; monitor if already low.

name: Mahkota Dewa (Phaleria macrocarpa)
mechanism: Traditional Southeast Asian herb with reported metabolic, vascular and libido effects.
notes: Treat as experimental; quality control critical.

name: Ononis spinosa / Osul Iepurelui
mechanism: Traditional diuretic and uro-genital herb; may influence pelvic microcirculation and fluid balance.
dose_range: Per herbal protocol (e.g., tea or extract)
notes: Romanian/Eastern European folk usage; limited modern data.

name: Ginkgo biloba
mechanism: Improves microcirculation and may enhance cognitive performance and sensory processing.
notes: Watch for interactions with anticoagulants.

name: Lithium (microdose)
mechanism: Very low-dose lithium (e.g., orotate) for neurotrophic and mood-stabilizing effects.
notes: Avoid high doses; monitor kidney function if chronic.

name: Enclomiphene
mechanism: Selective estrogen receptor modulator that increases LH/FSH and endogenous testosterone.
notes: Prescription drug; requires monitoring of labs and symptoms.

name: hCG (Human Chorionic Gonadotropin)
mechanism: Mimics LH to stimulate Leydig cells and testosterone production; preserves testicular volume.
notes: Prescription; use only with endocrine oversight.

name: Testosterone (exogenous)
mechanism: Exogenous testosterone replacement or enhancement; increases androgen levels and secondary sex characteristics.
dose_range: Dose per TRT or enhancement protocol
notes: Included as reference agent; your case focuses on endogenous modulation.

name: Choline (general)
mechanism: Essential nutrient but in this context discussed as promoting reabsorption of bile-bound toxins and increasing TMAO.
dose_range: Variable; not recommended in current protocol
notes: Tracked as a 'red list' agent for APv1.0 despite general nutritional role.

name: Lecithin
mechanism: Phosphatidylcholine-rich mixture; here associated with increased bile-metabolite recycling and potential mood effects.
dose_range: Variable; not recommended in current protocol
notes: Handled as negative/avoid in this specific context.

name: Ox bile
mechanism: Supplemental bile salt complex; discussed as potentially worsening bile-bound toxin reabsorption in cholestatic states.
notes: May be useful in other contexts, but we flag as avoid for suspected cholestasis.

name: Fermented Foods (kefir, sauerkraut, etc.)
mechanism: Live-culture foods supplying diverse microbes and postbiotics for gut and immune modulation.
notes: Includes kefir, yogurt, sauerkraut, etc.

name: Black Coffee
mechanism: Caffeine and polyphenols that stimulate bile dumping, lipolysis and adenosine antagonism.
notes: Core tool in Phase 1 bile-dump strategy.

name: Kefir Microbiota
mechanism: Complex multi-strain microbial community from kefir grains; seeds gut with lactic acid bacteria and yeasts.
notes: Overlaps with fermented foods but logged separately as microbial agent.

name: Spore/SBO Probiotics (generic)
mechanism: Broad category of soil-based organism probiotics beyond specific brands like MegaSpore.
notes: CAS-PRO-01 covers specific SBOs; this is the generic category anchor.

name: Acacia Fiber
mechanism: Soluble fiber that feeds beneficial bacteria and improves stool quality and motility.
notes: Similar to inulin/PHGG; gentler in many people.

name: Sulforaphane (Broccoli Sprout Extract)
mechanism: Isothiocyanate that activates Nrf2, increases antioxidant defenses and supports eNOS and detox pathways.
notes: Can be stimulating or GI-irritating at high dose.

name: Angion Protocol v2
mechanism: Ischemia–reperfusion and mechanical stimuli to induce VEGF-driven microvascular remodeling and improved penile hemodynamics.
notes: Non-molecular technique logged as a CAS physical agent for completeness.

name: Glutathione (oral, generic)
mechanism: Phase II conjugation cofactor and redox buffer; supports mitochondrial protection and detox throughput once bile flow is adequate.
notes: Low systemic bioavailability; mainly GI antioxidant. Introduce only after Phase 1 terrain is stable.

name: Reduced Glutathione (liposomal)
mechanism: More bioavailable GSH form enhancing systemic and mitochondrial redox control during higher metabolic flux.
notes: Use only once gut–liver axis is solid. Synergistic with taurine and vitamin C.

name: Medical Ozone (autohemotherapy)
mechanism: Transient oxidative pulse to blood inducing Nrf2 and antioxidant enzymes; improves rheology and microcirculation.
notes: External medical adjunct only; not part of APv1.0 stack but may be overlaid once basics are in place.

name: 7,8-Dihydroxyflavone (7,8-DHF)
mechanism: Small-molecule TrkB agonist mimicking BDNF signaling; supports limbic, olfactory and sexual-circuit plasticity.
notes: Use as cyclical neuroplasticity tool (e.g. 10 days on / 10 off). Avoid stacking with other strong neurotrophics.

name: 4'-DMA-7,8-DHF
mechanism: Enhanced 7,8-DHF analog with improved TrkB activation and brain penetration in preclinical models.
notes: Deep-end experimental tool; if ever used, apply as a short, single-agent microcycle only in Phase 3.

name: Mucuna Pruriens (L-DOPA)
mechanism: High-bioavailability natural source of L-DOPA; directly raises dopamine synthesis, increasing libido, motivation and orgasm intensity.
notes: Potent dopaminergic; avoid combining with 9-me-BC on same day; cycle for receptor sensitivity.

name: Maca (Lepidium meyenii)
mechanism: Traditional Peruvian root supporting libido, mood and energy; may modulate dopamine and endocrine tone.
notes: Generally well tolerated; synergistic with tongkat, tribulus, muira puama.

name: Fadogia Agrestis
mechanism: Acts as LH mimetic in preclinical data, potentially increasing testosterone and libido.
notes: Potent; monitor for restlessness, tension, or sleep disruption; use in microcycles.

name: Bulbine Natalensis
mechanism: Traditional anabolic herb; may raise testosterone and lower estrogen; requires careful dosing to avoid hepatic stress.
notes: High potency; use only in controlled, short cycles with lab monitoring.

name: Muira Puama
mechanism: Enhances sexual response, dopamine tone and mental focus; mild adaptogen.
notes: Synergistic with maca, tongkat, tribulus.

name: Damiana
mechanism: Mild CNS stimulant and libido enhancer, supporting parasympathetic arousal.
notes: Useful but mild; complements vascular agents rather than replacing them.

name: Tribulus Terrestris
mechanism: Saponin (protodioscin)-rich herb with modest androgenic and libido-supportive properties.
notes: Mild testosterone support; often used as part of multi-herb libidostacks.

name: TTFD (Thiamine Tetrahydrofurfuryl Disulfide)
mechanism: High-penetrance B1 derivative; strongly activates PDH and carbohydrate oxidation, boosting ATP, dopamine synthesis and nerve conduction.
notes: Introduce after Phase 1.5 once gut/liver stable; titrate up watching for overstimulation.

name: Uridine Monophosphate
mechanism: Supports phospholipid synthesis, synapse formation and dopaminergic tone; part of modified Mr. Happy stack (without choline).
notes: Use with DHA; avoid choline co-loading in cholestasis context.

name: Sulbuthiamine
mechanism: Lipid-soluble thiamine dimer that enhances dopaminergic tone, libido, social and sexual drive.
notes: Use in short cycles (e.g. 5 days on / 2 off) in Phase 2β+; avoid early terrain phases.

name: Methylated B-Complex
mechanism: Provides methylated B12, B6 and folate to support neurotransmitter synthesis, methylation and energy metabolism.
notes: Introduce only once histamine and sleep are stable; may increase stimulation in sensitive users.

name: Tributyrin
mechanism: Butyrate precursor; gut barrier repair; microglia calming
notes: Phase 2A gut-brain axis support

name: Mucuna Pruriens
mechanism: Natural L-DOPA; dopamine and libido ignition
notes: Phase 2B microcycles

name: Magnesium β-Hydroxybutyrate (MHB)
mechanism: Magnesium salt of β-hydroxybutyrate; supports mitochondrial ATP, reduces neuroinflammation, shifts autonomic tone parasympathetic, mildly GABAergic, enhances dopaminergic efficiency and sensual calm.
notes: Use in Phase 2–3 only; evening or late afternoon dosing with food; avoid combining with alcohol or sedative drugs.

name: HMB (β-Hydroxy β-Methylbutyrate)
mechanism: Leucine metabolite; increases androgen receptor density, reduces cortisol, enhances dopaminergic signaling, improves NO, increases libido.
notes: Use with meals; avoid fasted; enhances libido and neurosexual function.

name: Methylene Blue (low-dose)
mechanism: Redox-active dye; enhances mitochondrial electron transport, increases ATP, modulates monoamine systems, neuroprotective.
notes: Avoid high doses; interacts with SSRIs/MAOIs; strong HFMP-2 and HFMP-6 amplifier.

name: Urolithin A
mechanism: Polyphenol metabolite that promotes mitophagy and mitochondrial renewal, improving muscle function and endurance.
notes: Useful for long-term mitochondrial rejuvenation and HFMP-6 support.

name: Niacinamide
mechanism: NAD+ precursor, anti-lipolytic, anti-inflammatory; supports thyroid and mitochondrial function.
notes: Peat-aligned; avoid very high doses with liver compromise.

name: Riboflavin (B2)
mechanism: Cofactor in mitochondrial energy production and thyroid hormone activation.
notes: Synergistic with niacinamide and thyroid support.

name: Cabergoline
mechanism: D2 receptor agonist; strongly suppresses prolactin; can markedly increase libido and erectile performance.
notes: Prescription only; monitor mood, valvular function; nuclear HFMP-4 lever.

name: Bromocriptine
mechanism: Ergoline derivative; D2 agonist that lowers prolactin and may improve libido and metabolic health.
notes: Prescription; shorter-acting alternative to cabergoline.

name: T2 (3,5-diiodothyronine)
mechanism: Non-classical thyroid hormone that increases mitochondrial respiration and fat oxidation with less cardiac stimulation than T3.
notes: Experimental; to be used only with thyroid-aware strategy and labs.

name: Natural Desiccated Thyroid (NDT)
mechanism: Combination of T4 and T3 from desiccated animal thyroid; supports systemic thyroid function.
notes: Requires careful titration and regular lab monitoring.

name: Liothyronine (T3)
mechanism: Active thyroid hormone; strongly increases metabolic rate and mitochondrial activity.
notes: High leverage but risky; requires expert management and labs.

name: Lugol's Iodine
mechanism: Iodine/iodide solution; supports thyroid hormone synthesis and breast/testicular tissue health.
notes: Use only if iodine deficient and no active autoimmunity.

name: Potassium Iodide
mechanism: Iodide salt used for iodine repletion and thyroid support.
notes: Same cautions as Lugol's; start low.

name: Niacin (flush)
mechanism: Nicotinic acid; induces histamine-mediated vasodilation and may enhance endothelial function and arousal.
notes: Use sparingly; can raise liver enzymes at high chronic doses.

name: DAO (Diamine Oxidase) supplement
mechanism: Exogenous diamine oxidase to support degradation of dietary histamine.
dose_range: Single or double capsule with high-histamine meals
notes: Useful when histamine overshoots or with histamine intolerance.

name: Phosphatidylserine
mechanism: Cortisol-modulating phospholipid; supports neuronal membrane function and may improve stress handling and cognition.
notes: Can reduce stress-induced cortisol elevations; supports HFMP-2 and HFMP-9.

name: Cocoa Flavanols (high-flavanol cocoa extract)
mechanism: Rich in epicatechin and related flavanols; improves endothelial function and NO bioavailability.
notes: Use low-sugar extracts; strong synergy with NO stack.

name: Pentoxifylline
mechanism: Improves red blood cell deformability and microcirculation; used in some ED and Peyronie's protocols.
notes: Off-label; potential HFMP-7 support for refractory microvascular issues.

name: Artichoke Extract (Cynarin)
mechanism: Bile flow enhancer and lipid-lowering agent; supports liver and gallbladder function.
notes: Useful addition for cholestasis-prone terrain.

name: Milk Thistle (Silymarin)
mechanism: Hepatoprotective flavonolignans; antioxidant and membrane-stabilizing effects on hepatocytes.
notes: Classic liver support; may be layered once bile flow is improved.

name: Hyaluronic Acid (oral)
mechanism: Supports joint and connective tissue hydration; may improve fascia and tissue resilience.
notes: Long-term fascia and joint support.

name: Silica (Orthosilicic Acid)
mechanism: Supports collagen and elastin synthesis; beneficial for skin, hair, vessels, and fascia.
notes: Synergistic with collagen and vitamin C.

name: Lithium (microdose)
mechanism: Low-dose lithium supports neurotrophic signaling, mood stability, and may modulate neurosteroid and BDNF pathways.
notes: Avoid in those with kidney disease or on interacting meds.

name: Undecylenic Acid
mechanism: Inhibits yeast–hyphal transition; reduces Candida virulence and acetaldehyde load in gut.
notes: Primary antifungal in cholestatic terrain. Start low to avoid strong die-off; maintain binders and 1–2+ BMs/day.
phase_dosage: Phase 1–2: per label, typically titrate from 1–2 caps/day up to 5–10 caps/day divided with meals.

name: Cholestyramine
mechanism: Strongly binds bile acids and associated toxins in the gut to prevent enterohepatic recirculation.
notes: Powerful but constipating bile acid binder. Strips fat-soluble vitamins; use only temporarily and support motility.
phase_dosage: PRN or short cycles: 4g once or twice daily, away from food/meds/supps by 2–3 hours.

name: Intranasal Insulin
mechanism: Delivers insulin directly to CNS via olfactory route; supports hippocampal and olfactory bulb metabolism.
notes: Advanced, post-ethical tool. Potential for hypoglycemia is low but monitor carefully; not part of base CPO.
phase_dosage: Experimental: 5–10 IU IN per session, 1–2×/day in limited cycles.

name: Danazol (low dose)
mechanism: Synthetic androgen that strongly suppresses SHBG production and alters gonadotropins.
notes: Nuclear option for very high SHBG not responsive to natural methods. Androgenic and lipid-side-effect risk.
phase_dosage: Specialist use only: e.g. 50–100mg/day short cycles under medical supervision.

name: Nystatin (oral, non-absorbed)
mechanism: Polyene antifungal that remains in gut lumen; reduces Candida without systemic liver load.
notes: Medical-prescription tool. Useful when strong gut Candida is suspected and liver needs protection.
phase_dosage: Typical: 500,000–1,000,000 units 2–4×/day as per medical guidance.

name: Endotelon (grape seed procyanidolic oligomers)
mechanism: Standardized grape seed procyanidolic oligomers; venotonic/vasculoprotective; supports microcirculation & capillary resistance; antioxidant
dose_range: 150–300 mg/day (often 150 mg BID; some regimens 20 days/month)
notes: Chronic endothelial/microvascular support; not a reliable acute pre-sex lever. Caution stacking with aspirin/anticoagulants (bruising/bleeding).

name: TMG (Trimethylglycine)
mechanism: Methyl donor via BHMT; supports homocysteine→methionine; buffers methyl drain (e.g., high NAD+ support); osmolyte.
notes: Distinct from Betaine HCl; start low; monitor homocysteine; may not suit methyl-sensitive individuals.

name: Royal Jelly (freeze-dried)
mechanism: Dietary pterins/biopterin input; supports BH4 pool and NO coupling; antioxidant/anti-inflammatory support (context-dependent).
notes: Allergy risk (bee products); watch histamine-type reactions.

name: Sepiapterin (BH4 salvage precursor)
mechanism: BH4 precursor (salvage pathway) to support NOS coupling and reduce uncoupled NO/peroxynitrite signaling (context-dependent).
notes: Advanced lever; avoid during unstable phases; monitor BP/headache/anxiety; discontinue if adverse effects.

name: Sapropterin (BH4; Rx)
mechanism: Synthetic BH4 used clinically (e.g., PKU); may support NOS coupling when BH4 depleted (medical supervision).
notes: Medical supervision recommended; monitor response and labs.

name: Hops extract (8‑Prenylnaringenin / 8‑PN)
mechanism: Potent phytoestrogen (8‑PN); included only in RESET branch per epigenetic/AR resensitization hypothesis.
notes: RESET TRACK ONLY — pair with methyl-buffer (TMG) + monitoring; avoid if estrogenic side effects.
